description: Database exposure attack surface: service discovery, default and reused credentials, remote access, anonymous access, and sensitive data enumeration. Works via SSH without pre-configured data_sources.yaml.

DE-01.label: db_service_listening
DE-01.description: A database service (MySQL/MariaDB on 3306, PostgreSQL on 5432, MongoDB on 27017, Redis on 6379, MSSQL on 1433) is listening on the target, confirming a database attack surface exists.
DE-01.evidence_hint: Run 'ss -tlnp' or 'netstat -tlnp' and check for listening sockets on ports 3306, 5432, 27017, 6379, 1433, 1521.

DE-02.label: db_version_disclosed
DE-02.description: Database engine version is obtainable — either from the login banner, SELECT VERSION(), or process cmdline — enabling targeted CVE selection.
DE-02.evidence_hint: Run 'mysql -V', 'psql --version', or 'mongod --version' locally, or connect and run SELECT VERSION().

DE-03.label: default_credentials_accepted
DE-03.description: The database accepts a well-known default credential pair: MySQL root with empty password, PostgreSQL postgres/postgres, MongoDB no auth, Redis no requirepass, or other vendor defaults.
DE-03.evidence_hint: Attempt: mysql -u root -h 127.0.0.1 --password='' -e 'SELECT 1'; psql -U postgres -h 127.0.0.1 -c 'SELECT 1'; redis-cli PING. Success response = realized.

DE-04.label: cred_reuse_success
DE-04.description: A credential pair harvested from another service on this target (SSH, web, OS) was accepted by the database, confirming credential reuse.
DE-04.evidence_hint: Take username/password pairs from HO-18 (harvested_credentials) wicket and test each against discovered DB service.

DE-05.label: remote_access_allowed
DE-05.description: The database bind address is not restricted to localhost (127.0.0.1 / ::1). It binds to 0.0.0.0 or a routable interface, making it directly reachable from outside the host.
DE-05.evidence_hint: Check 'ss -tlnp' for database port binding to 0.0.0.0 or non-loopback address; inspect my.cnf for bind-address, postgresql.conf for listen_addresses.

DE-06.label: databases_enumerable
DE-06.description: An authenticated (or unauthenticated) session can list available databases, confirming the attacker has read access to the database catalog.
DE-06.evidence_hint: Run SHOW DATABASES (MySQL), \l or SELECT datname FROM pg_database (PG), show dbs (Mongo), KEYS * (Redis) after obtaining access.

DE-07.label: sensitive_tables_visible
DE-07.description: Tables with names suggesting sensitive data (users, accounts, passwords, credentials, tokens, payment, credit_card, ssn, health) are visible in at least one database.
DE-07.evidence_hint: Run SHOW TABLES IN <db> (MySQL) or SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables (PG) and check for pattern-matched sensitive names.

DE-08.label: auth_disabled
DE-08.description: The database is configured with authentication disabled or completely open: MongoDB with --noauth or security.authorization: disabled; Redis with no requirepass and no bind restriction; MySQL with skip-grant-tables.
DE-08.evidence_hint: Inspect mongod.conf for security.authorization, redis.conf for requirepass, my.cnf for skip-grant-tables; attempt unauthenticated connection.

DE-09.label: db_config_readable
DE-09.description: Database configuration files (my.cnf, postgresql.conf, mongod.conf, redis.conf) are readable via SSH, exposing bind addresses, auth config, replication setup, and potentially embedded credentials.

DE-10.label: db_user_with_file_priv
DE-10.description: A MySQL/MariaDB user account has FILE privilege, enabling LOAD DATA INFILE / SELECT INTO OUTFILE attacks that can read arbitrary host files or write web shells.
DE-10.evidence_hint: Run SHOW GRANTS FOR CURRENT_USER(); or SELECT user, File_priv FROM mysql.user WHERE File_priv='Y'.

DE-11.label: db_user_with_super_priv
DE-11.description: The authenticated database user has SUPER or DBA-equivalent privilege, enabling configuration changes, user creation, and in MySQL FILE privilege escalation.
DE-11.evidence_hint: SHOW GRANTS FOR CURRENT_USER() — look for SUPER, ALL PRIVILEGES, GRANT OPTION. PG: SELECT rolsuper FROM pg_roles WHERE rolname=current_user.

db_default_creds_v1.label: Database Default Credential Access
db_default_creds_v1.description: Database service is listening, accepts a vendor default credential, and exposes the database catalog — attacker has authenticated read access with no prior knowledge.

db_cred_reuse_v1.label: Database Credential Reuse
db_cred_reuse_v1.description: Database service is listening and a credential harvested from another service (SSH, web login) was accepted — lateral movement within the host via credential reuse.

db_anonymous_access_v1.label: Unauthenticated Database Access
db_anonymous_access_v1.description: Database authentication is disabled and the service is listening — attacker has direct access to all databases and tables with no credentials required.

db_remote_exposure_v1.label: Database Remotely Accessible
db_remote_exposure_v1.description: Database binds to a non-loopback address and accepts default or reused credentials from a remote client — not just accessible from the host itself.

db_file_priv_escalation_v1.label: MySQL FILE Privilege Escalation
db_file_priv_escalation_v1.description: Authenticated MySQL/MariaDB session with FILE privilege — attacker can read /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, write webshells, or exfiltrate arbitrary files via LOAD DATA / SELECT INTO OUTFILE.

db_data_exfiltration_v1.label: Sensitive Table Data Exfiltration
db_data_exfiltration_v1.description: Database session with enumeration access reveals tables containing sensitive data (user credentials, tokens, PII) — direct exfiltration path.
